metadata.title: Comprehensive Radiographic Positioning Database
metadata.description: All radiographic positioning views of the human body - Baseline values for average-build adult on DR system
references: Bushong's Radiologic Science for Technologists, 13th Edition
Merrill's Atlas of Radiographic Positions & Procedures, 15th Edition
European Guidelines on Quality Criteria for Diagnostic Radiographic Images, 2018

items.name: Skull AP
items.note: 15° caudal central ray to avoid overlap of anterior cranial fossa with base
items.positioning: OML perpendicular to film, CR exits nasion and enters glabella

items.name: Skull PA
items.note: Preferred over AP for anterior fossa detail and less OID magnification
items.positioning: OML perpendicular, CR centered on nasion

items.name: Skull Lateral
items.note: Side closest to film is the affected side to minimize magnification
items.positioning: IPL perpendicular to film, CR centered on EAM

items.name: Skull Towne's (Fronto-occipital)
items.note: Demonstrates posterior fossa, semicircular canals, foramen magnum. 30° caudal angle
items.positioning: OML perpendicular, CR 30° caudal exiting forehead and entering occiput

items.name: Skull Submentovertex (SMV / Basal)
items.note: Demonstrates skull base, semicircular canals, foramen ovale. Requires full neck extension — contraindicated for cervical spine patients
items.positioning: IPL perpendicular, CR vertical from below chin upward

items.name: Skull Basal Axial (Haas Method)
items.note: Alternative to SMV when patient cannot fully extend the neck
items.positioning: 25° caudal ray from below the chin

items.name: Sella Turcica Lateral
items.note: Magnified view focused on sella turcica region. May be done without grid
items.positioning: Standard skull lateral with collimated field and CR centered on sella

items.name: Optic Foramen (Rhese Method)
items.note: 37° angle inferior and toward nose. No grid to reduce noise. Each side imaged separately
items.positioning: PA with head rotated 37° toward the affected side

items.name: Sinuses PA (Caldwell)
items.note: 15° caudal ray. No grid. Demonstrates anterior and frontal sinuses
items.positioning: OML perpendicular, nose and forehead on film

items.name: Sinuses Waters (OMM)
items.note: Best view for maxillary sinuses. 37° caudal ray. Chin on film, nose away
items.positioning: OML 37° to film, chin and nose touching film

items.name: Sinuses Lateral
items.note: No grid. Shows all sinuses in a single lateral view
items.positioning: IPL perpendicular, CR centered on nose

items.name: Sinuses Lateral (Upright)
items.note: Best for detecting fluid levels in sinuses — patient upright
items.positioning: Lateral with collimated field on sinuses

items.name: Facial Bones AP/PA
items.note: 15° central ray for AP or none for PA. Shows entire facial skeleton
items.positioning: OML perpendicular, CR centered on nasion

items.name: Facial Bones Lateral
items.note: Shows nose, maxillary sinus, zygomatic arch
items.positioning: IPL perpendicular, CR centered on nose

items.name: Zygomatic Arch Tangential
items.note: No grid. Ray tangent to the arch. Central or posterior ray depending on method
items.positioning: Head tilted so arch is away from film, ray passes tangentially

items.name: Nasal Bones Lateral
items.note: No grid. Very low exposure for fine detail
items.positioning: IPL perpendicular, tight collimation on nose

items.name: Nasal Bones AP (Waters Modified)
items.note: 15° caudal ray. No grid
items.positioning: Modified Waters position with collimated field on nose

items.name: Mandible AP
items.note: Shows body, angle, and ramus of the mandible
items.positioning: AML perpendicular to film, CR centered on chin

items.name: Mandible Lateral Oblique
items.note: Multiple oblique angles to cover entire mandible. Each side imaged at opposite angle
items.positioning: Head rotated 30-45°, CR from opposite side

items.name: Mandible PA
items.note: Clearly shows ramus and angle with reduced spine overlap
items.positioning: AML perpendicular, CR centered on chin

items.name: TMJ Lateral (Parma Method)
items.note: No grid. Both joints imaged in open and closed mouth (4 images). Very tight collimation
items.positioning: IPL perpendicular, CR on EAM, field approx 6×8 cm

items.name: TMJ AP (Towne Modified)
items.note: Shows condylar head in AP projection. Rarely used
items.positioning: Modified Towne position with collimated field on TMJ

items.name: Cervical Spine AP
items.note: Shoulders depressed as much as possible (weight cords if needed). 15-20° cephalad ray to open interspinous spaces
items.positioning: AML perpendicular, CR on C4, weights in hands to depress shoulders

items.name: Cervical Spine Lateral
items.note: SID 150 cm to reduce magnification distortion. Shoulders depressed maximally. Must show C1-C7 completely
items.positioning: Cervical spine level perpendicular to film, CR on C4

items.name: Cervical Spine Open Mouth (Odontoid)
items.note: Patient opens mouth as wide as possible and says 'ah' during exposure to prevent motion. Shows dens and C1-C2
items.positioning: AML perpendicular, CR through open mouth

items.name: Cervical Spine Fuchs Method
items.note: Alternative to Open Mouth when patient cannot open mouth. Ray passes under the chin
items.positioning: Chin extended maximally, CR from below chin at slight angle

items.name: Cervical Pillars AP
items.note: Demonstrates lateral pillars of cervical vertebrae. 15° cephalad ray
items.positioning: Like standard AP but focused on pillars

items.name: Cervical Spine Oblique
items.note: Demonstrates intervertebral foramina (Foramina Transversaria). Both sides imaged

items.name: Cervical Spine Lateral Flexion
items.note: Patient flexes head forward as much as possible. For instability assessment. SID 150 cm
items.positioning: Lateral with maximal flexion, CR on C4

items.name: Cervical Spine Lateral Extension
items.note: Patient extends head backward as much as possible. For instability assessment. SID 150 cm
items.positioning: Lateral with maximal extension, CR on C4

items.name: Cervicothoracic Swimmer's View
items.note: Shows C7-T1 transition when not visible on standard lateral. One arm raised, other behind back
items.positioning: Near arm raised above head, far arm behind back

items.name: Thoracic Spine AP
items.note: Suspended respiration at end expiration to reduce rib motion. Relatively long exposure due to part thickness. Central ray
items.positioning: CR on T7, spine centered in image

items.name: Thoracic Spine PA
items.note: Less magnification than AP but difficult for most patients

items.name: Thoracic Spine Lateral
items.note: Highest exposure in spine imaging due to large thickness. Suspended respiration
items.positioning: Both arms raised above head, CR on T7

items.name: Thoracic Spine Oblique
items.note: Demonstrates thoracic facet joints. Both sides imaged

items.name: Thoracic Spine Spot View
items.note: Focused view on specific vertebra(e) after identifying them on the survey image

items.name: Lumbar Spine AP
items.note: Suspended respiration. Feet inverted 15-20° to open interspinous space. CR on L3
items.positioning: Feet inverted, CR on L3 (iliac crest level)

items.name: Lumbar Spine Lateral
items.note: Highest mAs in most skeletal exams due to thickness. Knees slightly flexed to flatten lumbar lordosis

items.name: L5-S1 Spot Lateral
items.note: Focused on lumbosacral transition. L5-S1 often not clearly seen on full lateral

items.name: Lumbar Spine Oblique
items.note: Shows 'Scottie Dog' — facet joints. Both sides imaged (right and left)

items.name: Sacrum AP
items.note: 15° cephalad ray to align sacral surfaces. CR 2-3 cm below umbilicus
items.positioning: Sacrum level parallel to film with cephalad ray

items.name: Sacrum Lateral
items.note: Difficult due to pelvis and femur overlap. Requires precise positioning

items.name: Coccyx AP
items.note: 10-15° caudal ray (opposite of sacrum). CR 3-4 cm posterior to symphysis pubis
items.positioning: Coccyx level parallel to film with caudal ray

items.name: Coccyx Lateral
items.note: Same difficulty as sacrum lateral

items.name: Sternum Lateral
items.note: SID 180 cm to reduce scatter noise from soft tissues. End of deep inspiration. Patient slightly anterior oblique
items.positioning: 15-20° anterior oblique, SID 180 cm, CR on mid-sternum

items.name: Sternum RAO Oblique
items.note: No grid because sternum is parallel to ray. End inspiration. 15-20° rotation. No AEC

items.name: Sternum LAO Oblique
items.note: Alternative to RAO. No grid. Same principle with opposite rotation

items.name: Upper Ribs PA
items.note: SID 180 cm. End of deep inspiration. CR on affected rib
items.positioning: Like chest PA with collimated field on ribs

items.name: Upper Ribs AP
items.note: For patients unable to stand

items.name: Upper Ribs Oblique (Anterior)
items.note: Separates anterior upper ribs. 45° rotation

items.name: Lower Ribs PA
items.note: SID 180 cm. End expiration to reduce diaphragm overlap. Lower ribs 8-12
items.positioning: Like chest PA with collimated field on ribs

items.name: Lower Ribs AP
items.note: End expiration

items.name: Lower Ribs Oblique
items.note: 45° rotation with end expiration

items.name: Chest PA
items.note: Gold standard for chest. SID 180 cm. End of deep inspiration. CR on T7. Shoulders anterior, hands on hips
items.positioning: Standing, hands on hips, shoulders forward, CR on T7

items.name: Chest AP
items.note: For patients unable to stand. Heart appears larger due to magnification. Slightly higher mAs than PA
items.positioning: Supine or seated, SID 180 cm if possible

items.name: Chest Lateral
items.note: Left side usually close to film (Left Lateral Decubitus for effusion). End of deep inspiration
items.positioning: Both arms raised above head, CR on T7

items.name: Chest AP Lordotic
items.note: Demonstrates lung apices and supraclavicular region clearly. Patient leans back from shoulders
items.positioning: Patient leaning back 15-20° from shoulders, abdomen on film

items.name: Chest Lateral Decubitus
items.note: For detecting pleural effusion (fluid shifts with gravity). Affected side up. Horizontal ray

items.name: Chest AP Decubitus
items.note: For detecting free air under diaphragm. Horizontal ray

items.name: Chest AP Portable (Bedside)
items.note: SID only 100 cm. No breathing control. Higher mAs to compensate for short SID and free breathing. Usually manual
items.positioning: Patient in bed, film behind back, SID 100 cm

items.name: Chest Routine (PA + Lat)
items.note: Average of PA and Lateral values for reference only. In practice, each view uses its own specific factors

items.name: Abdomen AP (KUB)
items.note: Suspended respiration at end expiration. May need short exposure (0.3-0.5 s) to reduce bowel motion. Usually includes upper pelvis
items.positioning: Supine, CR on iliac crest, field from thoracic cage to pubic symphysis

items.name: Abdomen Erect AP
items.note: Essential for detecting free air under diaphragm and air-fluid levels. Wait 5-10 minutes standing before imaging

items.name: Abdomen Lateral Decubitus
items.note: Alternative to erect abdomen when patient cannot stand. Right side usually down. Horizontal ray

items.name: KUB (Kidneys, Ureters, Bladder)
items.note: Same as abdomen AP but focused on urinary tract. No bowel prep in most cases

items.name: Abdomen Oblique
items.note: Separates kidneys from spine. Both sides imaged

items.name: Pelvis AP
items.note: Feet inverted 15-20° to show femoral neck axes. CR on symphysis pubis

items.name: Pelvis Inlet (Brim View)
items.note: Shows pelvic inlet and its dimensions. 40° caudal ray

items.name: Pelvis Outlet
items.note: Shows pelvic outlet. 35° cephalad ray from below pubis

items.name: Pelvis Right Judet Oblique
items.note: Shows right posterior pelvic column (iliac wing and acetabulum). 45° rotation

items.name: Pelvis Left Judet Oblique
items.note: Shows left posterior pelvic column. 45° rotation

items.name: SI Joints AP
items.note: Shows sacroiliac joints in general. 15-20° central ray
items.positioning: Like pelvis AP with collimated field on SI joints

items.name: Right SI Joint Oblique
items.note: Clearly shows right SI joint. 25-30° rotation. Affected side close to film
items.positioning: 25-30° rotation to the right (right joint on film)

items.name: Left SI Joint Oblique
items.note: Clearly shows left SI joint. 25-30° rotation
items.positioning: 25-30° rotation to the left (left joint on film)

items.name: Hip AP
items.note: Foot inverted 15-20° to show full femoral neck. CR 2.5 cm inferior and medial to greater trochanter

items.name: Hip Lateral (Frog-leg)
items.note: Knees flexed with soles together. Shows femoral neck well if patient can move
items.positioning: Knees flexed, soles together, CR on hip

items.name: Hip Lateral (Danelius-Miller)
items.note: Alternative to Frog-leg when patient cannot flex knee. Affected leg straight, other raised
items.positioning: Affected leg straight, other raised and flexed

items.name: Hip Lateral Cross-table
items.note: Best for injured hip (no movement). Horizontal ray. Film vertical beside hip. Manual technique
items.positioning: Supine, film beside hip, horizontal ray

items.name: Hip Oblique
items.note: Separates femoral neck from iliac branch

items.name: Shoulder AP Internal Rotation
items.note: Palm of hand on abdomen. Clearly shows greater tubercle and surgical neck

items.name: Shoulder AP External Rotation
items.note: Palm outward. Shows lesser tubercle and greater tuberosity. Usually imaged with internal rotation

items.name: Shoulder AP Neutral Rotation
items.note: Arm at side in neutral. Shows humerus in intermediate position

items.name: Shoulder AP Axillary (Lawrence)
items.note: Shows relationship of greater tubercle to glenoid cavity. Ray passes through axilla. Simplest axial method
items.positioning: Arm slightly elevated, ray from axilla upward

items.name: Shoulder Inferior Axillary (Velpeau)
items.note: Alternative when arm cannot be raised. Patient leans back 30° on film, ray from above shoulder downward

items.name: Shoulder Scapular Y Lateral
items.note: Shows position of greater tubercle relative to glenoid cavity. Y-shape from scapula, clavicle, and humerus
items.positioning: 45° rotation with scapula parallel to ray

items.name: Shoulder Stryker Notch View
items.note: Demonstrates Hill-Sachs lesions. Palm on head, 10° cephalad ray
items.positioning: Palm of hand on head, 10° cephalad ray

items.name: Clavicle AP
items.note: No grid usually. 15° cephalad ray to separate clavicle from thoracic spine

items.name: Clavicle PA
items.note: Better than AP for detail since clavicle is closer to film. No grid

items.name: Clavicle AP 15° Cephalad
items.note: Best position to separate clavicle from ribs and spine

items.name: AC Joint AP Unweighted
items.note: No grid. No weights. First imaged without weights then with weights for comparison

items.name: AC Joint AP Weighted
items.note: 5-10 kg weights in each hand. Detects partial AC joint separation
items.positioning: Same as AP unweighted but holding weights

items.name: Scapula AP
items.note: Arm rotated slightly outward to separate scapula from ribs

items.name: Scapula Lateral Y
items.note: Scapula parallel to ray (45° rotation). Shows spine, angle, and border

items.name: Humerus AP
items.note: Include shoulder and elbow if possible. External rotation of shoulder

items.name: Humerus Lateral
items.note: Elbow flexed 90°, lateral side on film

items.name: Humerus Oblique
items.note: Separates fracture from surrounding tissues

items.name: Elbow AP
items.note: No grid. Arm fully extended. CR on joint. Include part of forearm and humerus

items.name: Elbow Lateral
items.note: No grid. Elbow flexed 90°. Palm anterior. Lateral third of radial head should appear posterior to capitellum

items.name: Elbow Medial Oblique
items.note: No grid. Shows medial epicondyle and coronoid process. 45° internal rotation

items.name: Elbow Lateral Oblique
items.note: No grid. Shows radial head and lateral epicondyle. 45° external rotation

items.name: Forearm AP
items.note: No grid. Includes radius, ulna, elbow, and wrist. Palm up

items.name: Forearm Lateral
items.note: No grid. Elbow flexed 90°, palm anterior. Heel of hand on film

items.name: Wrist PA
items.note: No grid. Best position for fine detail (carpal bones). Hand flat, wrist joint centered. 10 cm from fingertip
items.positioning: Palm down on film, CR on wrist joint

items.name: Wrist Lateral
items.note: No grid. Wrist in neutral position (0°). Shows scaphoid-capitate

items.name: Wrist Medial Oblique
items.note: No grid. 45° medial rotation. Separates lateral carpal bones

items.name: Wrist Lateral Oblique
items.note: No grid. 45° lateral rotation

items.name: Wrist Scaphoid View (Ulnar Deviation PA)
items.note: No grid. Ulnar deviation extends and clearly shows scaphoid. Always required when scaphoid fracture is suspected
items.positioning: PA with maximal ulnar deviation, collimated field on scaphoid

items.name: Wrist Carpal Tunnel (Axial / Gaynor-Hart)
items.note: No grid. 25-30° cephalad ray from distal end. Shows carpal arch

items.name: Hand PA
items.note: No grid. Best position for fine detail. Fingers slightly spread. CR on 3rd metacarpal head
items.positioning: Palm down on film, fingers slightly spread

items.name: Hand Lateral
items.note: No grid. Lateral side on film

items.name: Hand Oblique
items.note: No grid. 45° rotation. Separates carpal bones and interphalangeal joints

items.name: Hand Norgaard (Ball-Catcher's View)
items.note: No grid. Shows metacarpal heads for early rheumatoid arthritis detection. Ball-catcher position
items.positioning: Palm down with fingers flexed as if catching a ball

items.name: Thumb AP (Robert's Method)
items.note: No grid. Thumb extended with internal hand rotation

items.name: Thumb Lateral
items.note: No grid. Thumb palm on film

items.name: Finger AP
items.note: No grid. Very tight collimation on affected finger. Other fingers extended away
items.positioning: Finger extended on film, collimated field

items.name: Finger Lateral
items.note: No grid. Collimated field. Adjacent finger extended as support

items.name: Finger Oblique
items.note: No grid. Collimated field. 45° rotation

items.name: Femur AP
items.note: Include hip and knee if possible. Foot in neutral position

items.name: Femur Lateral
items.note: Lower leg flexed. May need two images for long fractures

items.name: Femur Oblique
items.note: Separates fracture from surrounding tissues

items.name: Knee AP
items.note: Joint centered. Foot neutral. Both knees often imaged together for comparison

items.name: Knee Lateral
items.note: Knee flexed 20-30° to extend ligaments and project patella. Fibula posterior to tibia

items.name: Knee PA (Rosenberg / Weight-bearing)
items.note: Weight-bearing standing. 45° flexion. Best for evaluating joint space narrowing

items.name: Knee Medial Oblique
items.note: Clearly shows medial condyle

items.name: Knee Lateral Oblique
items.note: Clearly shows lateral condyle

items.name: Knee Intercondylar Notch (Tunnel View)
items.note: Shows intercondylar notch and loose bodies. 40-50° cephalad ray
items.positioning: 40° flexion, 40-50° cephalad ray from distal end

items.name: Patella Axial (Merchant / Skyline View)
items.note: No grid. Knee flexed 45°. 30° cephalad ray. Shows patellar articular surface and tracking
items.positioning: 45° flexion, 30° cephalad ray from distal end

items.name: Patella Axial (Settegast Method)
items.note: No grid. Alternative to Merchant. Patient prone with knee flexed

items.name: Patella Lateral
items.note: No grid. Collimated field on patella. Detects patellar fractures
items.positioning: Lateral with collimated field on patella

items.name: Lower Leg AP
items.note: Include knee and ankle if possible. Foot in neutral position

items.name: Lower Leg Lateral
items.note: Full lateral position

items.name: Lower Leg Oblique
items.note: Separates tibia from fibula

items.name: Ankle AP
items.note: No grid. Foot in neutral (90°). Shows talocrural joint

items.name: Ankle Lateral
items.note: No grid. Lateral side on film

items.name: Ankle Mortise View (15-20° Internal Rotation)
items.note: No grid. Most important for ankle injuries. 15-20° internal rotation eliminates fibula overlap. Clearly shows joint space

items.name: Ankle Stress Eversion
items.note: No grid. Eversion force applied to test deltoid ligament tear. May require local anesthesia

items.name: Ankle Stress Inversion
items.note: No grid. Inversion force applied to test lateral ligament tear

items.name: Foot AP (Dorsoplantar)
items.note: No grid. Foot vertical perpendicular to film. CR on 3rd metatarsal base

items.name: Foot Lateral
items.note: No grid. Lateral side on film

items.name: Foot Medial Oblique
items.note: No grid. 30-45° internal rotation. Separates cuboid and cuneiform bones

items.name: Foot Lateral Oblique
items.note: No grid. 30-45° external rotation

items.name: Calcaneus Axial (Harris-Beath)
items.note: No grid. Axial ray from posterior at 40° cephalad. Shows calcaneus anterior to posterior. Essential for calcaneal fractures
items.positioning: Foot extended, 40° cephalad ray from posterior

items.name: Calcaneus Lateral
items.note: No grid. Shows Boehler's angle. Complementary to axial view
items.positioning: Lateral with collimated field on calcaneus

items.name: Foot Sesamoid Axial
items.note: No grid. Axial ray from above foot anteriorly at angle. Shows sesamoid bones under 1st metatarsal head
items.positioning: Axial ray from above 1st metatarsal head

items.name: Foot Cuboid Oblique
items.note: No grid. Specific rotation to demonstrate cuboid bone

items.name: Thoracic Outlet (Cervicothoracic)
items.note: Shows cervicothoracic transition. SID 180 cm
items.positioning: Like chest PA with collimated field on thoracic outlet

items.name: Scoliosis Full Spine AP
items.note: SID 180 cm. Long film (14×36 inch). Manual technique. End inspiration. Also image lateral. Cobb angle measured
items.positioning: Standing, long film from C1 to sacrum

items.name: Scoliosis Full Spine Lateral
items.note: SID 180 cm. Highest mAs due to large thickness. Manual technique. Long film

items.name: Leg Length Measurement (Orthoroentgenogram)
items.note: Three exposures on single film: at hip, knee, and ankle. Metal ruler fixed beside leg. Manual technique
items.positioning: Supine, metal ruler beside leg, three exposures

items.name: Skeletal Survey (Pediatric)
items.note: Series of images for detecting child abuse. Each image uses its own specific factors. This value is approximate average. No grid for most images. Strict ALARA principle

thin.description: تقليل kVp بمقدار 6 وتقليل mAs بنسبة 45% بسبب قلة امتصاص الأنسجة

normal.description: القيم الأساسية كما هي دون تعديل

fat.description: زيادة kVp بمقدار 10 وزيادة mAs بنسبة 70% بسبب زيادة امتصاص الأنسجة

dr.name: Digital Radiography (Flat Panel)
dr.description: Highest detection efficiency — baseline values are based on this

cr.name: Computed Radiography (IP Plates)
cr.description: Lower efficiency than DR by approximately 50% — requires more mAs

notes.gridUsage: Grid is typically used when the part thickness exceeds 10 cm. Never used for extremities or pediatric patients.
notes.aecUsage: AEC is useful for chest, abdomen, pelvis, and spine. Usually manual for extremities. Always manual for axial views.
notes.sidStandard: Standard distance is 100 cm. Increased to 150 cm for cervical lateral (reduce magnification distortion) and 180 cm for chest (reduce cardiac magnification).
notes.breathingGuide: suspended = suspended respiration (most skeletal exams) | inspiration = end of deep inspiration (chest) | expiration = end of expiration (lower ribs) | free = free breathing (extremities)
notes.alaraPrinciple: ALARA: As Low As Reasonably Achievable — minimize radiation dose as much as possible, especially for children and pregnant women.
notes.pediatricWarning: All pediatric values are approximate and should be adjusted based on actual weight. Strongly recommend using the pediatric protocols established in each department.
notes.clinicalJudgment: Final values are the responsibility of the supervising radiologist and should be adjusted based on each case's specific circumstances.
